compliance_gate.robots_txt: All three hosts serve 'User-agent: * Disallow: /'. n.news.naver.com additionally prohibits AI-training/RAG bot access in-file; only Facebook/Twitter preview bots are allowed anywhere.
compliance_gate.api_access_control: Comment JSONP API rejects requests without an article-page Referer (success:false, code 3999). Both APIs are private, undocumented Naver internals.
compliance_gate.verdict: Live collection violates robots.txt and likely Naver terms, and the comment payload contains PIPA-scoped personal data. DO NOT implement a live collector. Fixtures below are for OFFLINE schema/consumer tests only, pending an explicit legal/compliance decision.
compliance_gate.credentials: None used, none required, none permitted. Never store guestToken, cookies, or session headers.

article_page.rendering: server-rendered shell, UTF-8; comment count NOT in static HTML

static_fields.comment_ui_anchor: element id 'comment_count' exists but text is the label '댓글' only (no number statically)

comment_page.rendering: JS skeleton only; zero comment content or counts in static HTML
comment_page.action: do not scrape; contains nothing statically

verified_params.templateId: default_society (accepted regardless of article category in probe)

comment_aggregate_api.availability_flag: result.exposureConfig.status == 'COMMENT_ON' means comments enabled; any other status = unavailable (non-COMMENT_ON values unverified)
comment_aggregate_api.prohibited_response_sections: result.commentList and result.bestList in their entirety (contain userName, maskedUserId/Name, userIdNo, profileUserId, userProfileImage, exposedUserIp, contents, mentions, replyList — PIPA-scoped personal data)

reaction_api.required_header: none verified (works without Referer and without login)
reaction_api.aggregate_fields: contents[].reactions[].{reactionType,count}; observed news reaction types: useful, wow, touched, recommend (like/warm/sad/angry family exists on other content types)

article_comment_metadata_fixture.description: Sanitized ARTICLE-LEVEL aggregate snapshot for offline schema/consumer tests. One record per article; never contains any per-comment or per-user data.

sanitization_rules: Store ONLY the article-level aggregate fields defined above; drop everything else from API responses at parse time.
Never store any element of commentList or bestList — no comment bodies, no per-comment counts, no commentNo/sortValue, no user fields, no IPs, even masked.
Never store guestToken, cookies, callback names, or raw request/response headers.
Never store raw JSONP/HTML payloads in fixtures; store only the derived record schema above.
Always record fetched_at and source_urls so staleness is auditable.
Preserve publisher attribution (publisher name + original article URL) in every record.
Fixtures live under test fixtures only; they must not feed production content generation or ranking.
known_risks: Both endpoints are unversioned private internals; params (ticket/templateId/pool) live in JS bundles and can change or be access-restricted without notice — any consumer must fail soft into the project's fallback chain.
Comment payload is one field away from personal data; a parsing mistake could persist PII. The drop-at-parse-time rule above is mandatory.
robots.txt fully disallows crawling on all three hosts; this contract does not authorize live collection.
Behavior for comments-disabled articles (exposureConfig statuses other than COMMENT_ON) is unverified.
The official Naver Open API offers no comment/reaction data, so there is no sanctioned migration path for this signal.
